title: Research VS Code live tab-style autocomplete options
scope: Find and evaluate VS Code options that can replace GitHub Copilot-style inline/tab autocomplete. Include commercial, open-source, local-model, and API-key based approaches. Focus on compatibility with OpenAI-compatible APIs, Anthropic APIs, local models, and whether they can use ChatGPT/Claude subscriptions directly.
goals: Cover open-source projects such as Continue, Tabby, twinny, and other credible current candidates.
Include credible commercial baselines such as Tabnine, Codeium/Windsurf, Supermaven, Sourcegraph Cody, and any VS Code fork or non-extension product only if clearly marked as near-scope.
Evaluate inline/FIM support, acceptance UX, latency expectations, and context handling rather than generic chat quality.
Include extension marketplace/GitHub/docs URLs, maintenance signals, licensing, provider support, and whether inline suggestions are supported.
Identify setup complexity, expected UX quality, privacy posture, and cost model.
Flag unsupported or speculative options clearly.
non_goals: Do not research Obsidian except when a VS Code option has a cross-editor path.
Do not write the final report.
safety: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or prompt-buffer.md.
Do not edit files tagged human-written.
Use current web sources for product status.
success_criteria: Include a ranked option list with evidence, pros, cons, and confidence.
